Source: Shadow Trace Ops Identity KQL Pack
WhyItMatters: Identity telemetry helps validate whether authentication was expected, risky, from unmanaged devices, from rare locations, or using legacy clients.
ReviewSteps: Review all sign-ins for the target user in the selected lookback.
Check failed sign-ins and risk indicators.
Review device trust and compliance context.
Identify legacy client/basic auth style patterns where schema supports it.
Review rare countries or locations compared to user baseline.
Pivot into IdentityLogonEvents when available for deeper identity-provider context.
ExpectedResults: Rows may show sign-in activity, failures, risk, unmanaged/non-compliant device use, legacy client patterns, rare locations, or identity-provider logon events.
NoResultsMeaning: No matching identity telemetry was returned, or the relevant Entra/identity Advanced Hunting table is unavailable in the tenant.
RecommendedActions: Validate whether the sign-in activity is expected.
Review Conditional Access and session controls for unmanaged devices.
Investigate risky sign-ins and rare locations.
Block or restrict legacy clients where appropriate.
Use Entra admin center and Defender XDR to validate findings before remediation.
